summary: Iteration 7 testing complete: Audit Logs (backend + frontend) and Extended Push Notifications integration. 16/16 backend tests passing; frontend Audit Logs page renders correctly for admin with stats, filters, table, and pagination ready. Push extension confirmed non-breaking on services/reportes flows.
critical_code_review_comments: audit_service.py creates a new AsyncIOMotorClient on EVERY log_action call (and audit_logs.py route also does it). This leaks Mongo connections under load. Recommend a single shared client/db handle from server.py.
routes/audit_logs.py is missing a trailing slash variant; the route is registered as '' which works but please verify ingress routing under '/api/audit-logs'.

AuditLog.timestamp is stored as ISO string (doc['timestamp'] = ...isoformat()) — sorting by string works for UTC ISO but loses BSON date semantics; consider storing as native datetime for future range queries.
Frontend AuditLogs.js: action filter is a free-text input that triggers a new request on every keystroke (no debounce). Consider debouncing for production traffic.

Pagination buttons only render when totalPages > 1 — acceptable, but with 24 total logs and pageSize 50 the buttons are hidden. That's expected but ensure tests don't assert their visibility unconditionally.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed services (ubicacion=en_local) and one reporte per run for verifying audit logs. Services are anulados at the end of TestPushExtensionFlows. Test reportes/services are not deleted afterwards; admin can purge by caso_numero pattern if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-7 features are working. Backend pytest file is /app/backend/tests/test_audit_logs_iteration7.py and can be re-run as regression. AuditLogs frontend has data-testid coverage: audit-logs-page, audit-accion-filter, audit-entidad-filter, audit-prev-page, audit-next-page. 403-for-non-admin was validated only via unauthenticated (no test passwords available for tecnico/asesor users; their credentials are not in test_credentials.md). Verified push-sending endpoints (POST /services, PUT /aprobar, PUT /anular, POST /agregar-item, POST /reportes) all return correct status codes despite no real subscriptions. Console shows React 'span cannot be child of select/tbody' hydration warnings — these come from the platform's dev-mode instrumentation (data-ve-dynamic spans injected for source tracing), NOT from AuditLogs.js code. Safe to ignore in this environment.
